title: EccKey::create correctly flushes srk on every failure path — defend against 'simplification'
description: key.rs EccKey::create: after wolfTPM2_GetKeyTemplate_ECC fails (step 2), srk is already loaded. The code explicitly calls wolfTPM2_UnloadHandle(srk) before returning Err. Same after wolfTPM2_CreateAndLoadKey fails (step 3). This prevents TPM transient slot leaks. A future contributor who refactors create() with early returns via ? without preserving these cleanup paths would silently leak TPM object slots. The explicit Err-path cleanup should be kept and a comment should note that Rust's Drop does not run for partially-constructed values.
